steps.title: Rearrange the equation
steps.content:
Start with the equation $x^2 - \frac{49}{16} = 0$. Add $\frac{49}{16}$ to both sides:
$$x^2 = \frac{49}{16}$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides:
$$x = \pm\sqrt{\frac{49}{16}} = \pm\frac{7}{4}$$
This gives exactly two distinct real solutions: $x = \frac{7}{4}$ and $x = -\frac{7}{4}$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
